channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Forget about Blu-ray, Fluo-ray discs may well be the future of optical data storage with 10TB capacities for $1
item.description: Optera Data is working on a new optical storage technology with its sights on 10TB capacities for as low as $1.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Apple finally lets you merge two Apple IDs—kinda. Here’s how
item.description: A few years ago, I chronicled the journey I went on to manually merge two Apple ID accounts into one.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: We're getting achingly closer to SOT-MRAM; the memory that could one day replace DRAM and NAND, but I can't see it happening soon
item.description: Researchers claim to have reached a goal that will allow SOT-MRAM to become reality sooner, just not too soon.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI turns down Musk's takeover bid
item.description: OpenAI’s board of directors has turned down Elon Musk’s $97.4 billion bid to buy the nonprofit that controls the artificial intelligence company. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: A partnership between Jigsaw and this Kentucky city could be the future of civic engagement
item.description: Bowling Green, Kentucky, is known for being the city from which Corvettes roll off the production lines, and for Fruit of the Loom underwear, which is headquartered there.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Treasury watchdog office is auditing DOGE’s access to federal government’s payment system
item.description: The Treasury Department’s Office of Inspector General on Friday said it was launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government’s payment system, after Democratic senators raised red flags about the access provided to Trump aide Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency team
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin to cut 10 percent of workforce
item.description: Jeff Bezos’s aerospace company Blue Origin plans to cut about 10 percent of its workforce, its CEO told staff Thursday, according to an email obtained by The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Buckle up for faster Python programs
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk says he'll withdraw $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI if ChatGPT maker remains nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk says he will abandon his $97.4 billion offer to buy the nonprofit behind OpenAI if the ChatGPT maker drops its plan to convert into a for-profit company. “If OpenAI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI revamps AI roadmap, merging models for a leaner future
item.description: OpenAI will integrate “o3” into GPT-5 instead of releasing it separately, streamlining adoption while signaling a shift toward fewer, more controlled AI models amid rising competition and cost pressures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Diving into the Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Announced at the May 2024 launch of Arm-powered Copilot+ PCs, the Windows Copilot Runtime is at the heart of Microsoft’s push to bring AI inferencing out from Azure and on to the edge and our laptops.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: EU pulls back – for the moment – on privacy and genAI liability compliance regulations
item.description: When the EU on Tuesday said it was not, at this time, moving ahead with critical legislation involving privacy and genAI liability issues, it honestly reported that members couldn’t agree. But the reasons why they couldn’t agree get much more complicated.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CISPE raises red alert over Broadcom’s VMware licensing changes
item.description: The European Cloud Competition Observatory (ECCO), a monitoring body established by the association of Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe (CISPE) as a way to keep cloud licensing practices fair in the region, this week came down hard on Broadcom for its failure to do so.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: House Commerce chair launches data privacy working group
item.description: House Committee on Energy and Commerce Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) on Wednesday announced the creation of a data privacy working group as policymakers work toward a national privacy standard.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Treasury Department revokes access 'mistakenly' given to Musk aide
item.description: A former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer "mistakenly” received editing access to a sensitive federal payment system at the Treasury Department for a brief period last week before staff revoked his capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: HPE expands ProLiant server portfolio, boosts AI and security features
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) added eight new servers to its ProLiant Gen 12 server portfolio, bringing advanced security and AI-optimization features to enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI company Ross Intelligence loses copyright fight with Thomson Reuters
item.description: A US judge has ruled in favor of Thomson Reuters in a AI training fight against Ross Intelligence, a legal AI startup, according to The Verge. Thomson Reuters sued Ross Intelligence in 2020 for using the company’s legal research platform Westlaw to train Ross Intelligence’s AI without permission.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: VP calls for ideology-free AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to not fall in love with AI-powered romance scammers
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how to protect yourself against artificial intelligence-fueled romance scams, which have become the most common type of fraud in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Yes, you can still upgrade Windows 10 PCs to Windows 11
item.description: Windows 10 has less than a year left before it hits its end of support deadline. Starting in October 2025, you’ll have to pay for security updates if you want to keep using Microsoft’s nearly-nine-year-old operating system.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cisco doubles down on AI with new, updated certifications
item.description: Cisco this week furthered its commitment to help customers support and develop artificial intelligence systems by rolling out new certification and training courses aimed at teaching professionals everything from how to incorporate AI into specific roles to advanced networking design.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta layoffs to begin as company moves to cut 5 percent of workforce
item.description: The layoffs, according to Zuckerberg, would target "low-performers." The layoffs will amount to roughly 3,600 people losing their jobs, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
